title: Black Sun Gospel
genre: Dark Soul · Blues-Rock · Cinematic Gospel · Slow-Burn DnB/Half-Time
concept: A gospel record for people who left the church but kept the fire — redemption with no theology attached, just scars, dignity and the chord changes that used to mean something.
statement: Choir responses and blues guitar over half-time breaks and cinematic strings — heroic without religion, raspy and powerful, built for survival rather than salvation.
visualIdentity: A black sun rising over scorched fields, work-worn hands, candle rows, deep red dust light.
typographyMood: Heavy, weighted serifs with worn edges — like letterpress run too many times. Uppercase used sparingly, for weight not decoration.
musicalStyle: Dark soul, blues-rock, cinematic gospel, slow-burn DnB/half-time breaks, orchestral pressure. Gospel choir responses, blues guitar, cinematic strings, heavy drums, sub bass, organ, brass, dark piano.
vocalDirection: Powerful raspy male or female contralto, wounded but heroic — no American country accent, no theatrical excess, dignity over spectacle.
